Paper_Metadata.Paper_Title: The impact of surface orientation on surface roughness and fatigue life of laser-based powder bed fusion Ti-6Al-4V

Process_Text.original: LB-PBF manufacturing using SLM Solutions 250 with Ti-6Al-4V powder. Two drying runs completed before using virgin powder to remove moisture. Powder sieved (-100 um) with Dv90 of 65.8 um and Dv50 of 44.5 um. Process parameters: 30 um layer height, 100 W laser power, 425 mm/s scan speed on borders, 375 mm/s scan speed on hatching. Specimens manufactured at 0 deg (horizontal) inclination angle. Support structures used for gauge section. HIP treatment: 820 degC, 200 MPa, 2 h hold time in pure argon atmosphere. Specimens ultrasonically cleaned using de-ionised water prior to HIP.
Process_Text.simplified: LB-PBF (SLM Solutions 250); 30 um layer height; 100 W laser power; 425 mm/s border, 375 mm/s hatch; 0 deg inclination; HIP 820 C/200 MPa/2h Ar;

Microstructure_Text.original: Alpha' martensite typical of LB-PBF Ti-6Al-4V observed before HIP. Needle-like alpha laths are observed within prior beta grains. Prior beta grains exhibit epitaxial growth parallel to the build direction. After HIP, grain coarsening occurs with lath length and breadth increasing by approximately two times. Area increases from 3.25±7.72 um2 to 10.7±18.4 um2. Lath length increases from 4.04±5.03 um to 7.86±8.02 um. Lath breadth increases from 1.19±0.71 um to 2.16±1.46 um. Aspect ratio increases from 3.07±1.73 to 3.60±2.12. Bulk region shows slightly larger lath dimensions than edge region (length 7.92 vs 7.66 um, breadth 2.14 vs 2.13 um).
Microstructure_Text.simplified: Alpha' martensite matrix with needle-like alpha laths. After HIP: grain coarsening with lath dimensions approximately doubling. Columnar prior beta grains in bulk regions.

Properties_Info.Test_Condition: at room temperature; stress ratio R=0.1; frequency 20 Hz
Properties_Info.Note: Exponential regression curve; Upward-facing specimens outperform downward-facing specimens by average 63% in cycles to failure

Specimens manufactured at 0 deg (horizontal) inclination angle. Support structures used for gauge section. HIP treatment: 820 degC, 200 MPa, 2 h hold time in pure argon atmosphere. Process_Text.simplified: LB-PBF (SLM Solutions 250); 30 um layer height; 100 W laser power; 425 mm/s border, 375 mm/s hatch; 0 deg inclination; HIP 820 C/200 MPa/2h Ar;

Microstructure_Text.original: Alpha' martensite typical of LB-PBF Ti-6Al-4V observed before HIP. Needle-like alpha laths within prior beta grains. HIP treatment causes grain coarsening: lath area increases from 3.25 to 10.7 um2, length from 4.04 to 7.86 um, breadth from 1.19 to 2.16 um.

Process_Text.original: LB-PBF manufacturing using SLM Solutions 250 with Ti-6Al-4V powder. Two drying runs completed before using virgin powder. Powder sieved (-100 um) with Dv90 of 65.8 um and Dv50 of 44.5 um. Process parameters: 30 um layer height, 100 W laser power, 425 mm/s scan speed on borders, 375 mm/s scan speed on hatching. Specimens manufactured at 10 deg inclination angle. Support structures required for gauge section. HIP treatment: 820 degC, 200 MPa, 2 h hold time in pure argon atmosphere. Specimens ultrasonically cleaned prior to HIP.
Process_Text.simplified: LB-PBF (SLM Solutions 250); 30 um layer height; 100 W laser power; 425 mm/s border, 375 mm/s hatch; 10 deg inclination; HIP 820 C/200 MPa/2h Ar;

Microstructure_Text.original: Alpha' martensite typical of LB-PBF Ti-6Al-4V observed before HIP. Needle-like alpha laths within prior beta grains. After HIP: grain coarsening occurs. For 30 deg specimens: lath area 10.9 um2 (bulk) / 9.23 um2 (edge); length 7.39 um (bulk) / 7.00 um (edge); breadth 2.23 um (bulk) / 2.10 um (edge). 10 deg specimens showed greatest surface roughness and worst fatigue performance due to stair stepping effect.
Microstructure_Text.simplified: Alpha' martensite matrix with needle-like alpha laths. After HIP: grain coarsening. 10 deg specimens exhibit worst surface roughness due to stair stepping (h ~ 173 um for 30 um layer thickness).

Process_Text.original: LB-PBF manufacturing using SLM Solutions 250 with Ti-6Al-4V powder. Two drying runs completed before using virgin powder. Powder sieved (-100 um) with Dv90 of 65.8 um and Dv50 of 44.5 um. Process parameters: 30 um layer height, 100 W laser power, 425 mm/s scan speed on borders, 375 mm/s scan speed on hatching. Specimens manufactured at 30 deg inclination angle. Support structures required for gauge section (below 40 deg). HIP treatment: 820 degC, 200 MPa, 2 h hold time in pure argon atmosphere. Specimens ultrasonically cleaned prior to HIP.
Process_Text.simplified: LB-PBF (SLM Solutions 250); 30 um layer height; 100 W laser power; 425 mm/s border, 375 mm/s hatch; 30 deg inclination; HIP 820 C/200 MPa/2h Ar;

Microstructure_Text.original: Alpha' martensite typical of LB-PBF Ti-6Al-4V observed before HIP. Needle-like alpha laths within prior beta grains. After HIP: grain coarsening occurs. For 30 deg specimens: lath area 10.9 um2 (bulk) / 9.23 um2 (edge); length 7.39 um (bulk) / 7.00 um (edge); breadth 2.23 um (bulk) / 2.10 um (edge); aspect ratio 3.23 (bulk) / 3.35 (edge). Stair step edge distance h ~ 60 um for Lt=30 um and alpha=30 deg.
Microstructure_Text.simplified: Alpha' martensite matrix with needle-like alpha laths. After HIP: grain coarsening. 30 deg specimens have larger lath breadth than 0 deg and 90 deg specimens.

Process_Text.original: LB-PBF manufacturing using SLM Solutions 250 with Ti-6Al-4V powder. Two drying runs completed before using virgin powder. Powder sieved (-100 um) with Dv90 of 65.8 um and Dv50 of 44.5 um. Process parameters: 30 um layer height, 100 W laser power, 425 mm/s scan speed on borders, 375 mm/s scan speed on hatching. Specimens manufactured at 50 deg inclination angle. No supports required in gauge section (above 40 deg). HIP treatment: 820 degC, 200 MPa, 2 h hold time in pure argon atmosphere. Specimens ultrasonically cleaned prior to HIP.
Process_Text.simplified: LB-PBF (SLM Solutions 250); 30 um layer height; 100 W laser power; 425 mm/s border, 375 mm/s hatch; 50 deg inclination; no supports in gauge section; HIP 820 C/200 MPa/2h Ar;

Microstructure_Text.original: Alpha' martensite typical of LB-PBF Ti-6Al-4V. Specimens manufactured at 50 deg inclination angle. Support structures not required in gauge section (above 40 deg). HIP treatment: 820 degC, 200 MPa, 2 h hold time in pure argon atmosphere. 50 deg upward-facing surface showed lowest surface roughness across all metrics (except Rv). Polished face Ra = 0.7 um +/- 0.1 um.
Microstructure_Text.simplified: Alpha' martensite matrix. 50 deg inclination; no support structures in gauge section; HIP treated.

Properties_Info.Test_Condition: at room temperature; stress ratio R=0.1; frequency 20 Hz; hydraulic MTS universal testing machine, 100 kN load cell
Properties_Info.Note: Inclination angle 50 deg; both upward and downward-facing surfaces tested; 50 deg specimens reached runout

Process_Text.original: LB-PBF manufacturing using SLM Solutions 250 with Ti-6Al-4V powder. Two drying runs completed before using virgin powder. Powder sieved (-100 um) with Dv90 of 65.8 um and Dv50 of 44.5 um. Process parameters: 30 um layer height, 100 W laser power, 425 mm/s scan speed on borders, 375 mm/s scan speed on hatching. Specimens manufactured at 90 deg (vertical) inclination angle. No supports required in gauge section. HIP treatment: 820 degC, 200 MPa, 2 h hold time in pure argon atmosphere. Specimens ultrasonically cleaned prior to HIP.
Process_Text.simplified: LB-PBF (SLM Solutions 250); 30 um layer height; 100 W laser power; 425 mm/s border, 375 mm/s hatch; 90 deg vertical orientation; no supports in gauge section; HIP 820 C/200 MPa/2h Ar;

Microstructure_Text.original: Alpha' martensite typical of LB-PBF Ti-6Al-4V observed before HIP. Needle-like alpha laths within prior beta grains. Prior beta grains exhibit epitaxial growth parallel to the build direction. After HIP: grain coarsening occurs with lath length and breadth increasing by approximately two times. Lath area 10.7 um2 (bulk) / 9.56 um2 (edge); length 7.86 um (bulk) / 7.46 um (edge); breadth 2.16 um (bulk) / 2.11 um (edge); aspect ratio 3.60 (bulk) / 3.49 (edge). Side-facing surface tested (90 deg vertical orientation).
Microstructure_Text.simplified: Alpha' martensite matrix with needle-like alpha laths. After HIP: grain coarsening with lath dimensions approximately doubling. 90 deg vertical orientation with side-facing surface.

Properties_Info.Note: Inclination angle 90 deg (vertical); side-facing surface tested; 90 deg specimens reached runout; failure initiation at corners or near edges

Process_Text.original: LB-PBF manufacturing using SLM Solutions 250 with Ti-6Al-4V powder. Two drying runs completed before using virgin powder. Powder sieved (-100 um) with Dv90 of 65.8 um and Dv50 of 44.5 um. Process parameters: 30 um layer height, 100 W laser power, 425 mm/s scan speed on borders, 375 mm/s scan speed on hatching.
